human_read: Promising but not S-tier. It reads as pressure, but not clearly enough before the major move.
what_improved: Area-gap object is visually calmer than V7/V8.
what_got_worse: Still feels somewhat detached from the actionable impulse/retest grammar.
next_iteration: V10 should make fill logic depend more on fast-vs-slow slope and main signal context.

human_read: Useful on this case; it shows a negative-to-positive pressure transition near the early April breakout.
what_improved: The wave turns before the move fully confirms.
what_got_worse: Still broad and may not isolate the exact trendline/wedge break the user sees.
next_iteration: Make fast wave more responsive near zero without making the whole wave jagged.

what_improved: Not scored. CRYPTOCAP:TURBO loaded price but blank wave pane; CRYPTO:TURBOUSD and CRYPTO:TURBOUSDC were not usable in this layout.
what_got_worse: Feed/layout issue surfaced by the matrix.
next_iteration: Cannot judge the candidate on this important case yet.
column18: Find the exact TradingView feed used in the user's TURBO screenshot before scoring.

human_read: Readable, but the wave behaves more like a broad cycle than a setup trigger.
what_improved: It smooths the messy oscillator into something inspectable.
what_got_worse: It may miss the user's key observation: time above viscosity and quick recovery after failure.
next_iteration: Add a pressure-persistence feature or visual accent for sustained time above viscosity.

human_read: Helpful for broad structural pressure but not precise enough around the actual downtrend-break reversal.
what_got_worse: Too slow to explain the exact early reversal grammar.
next_iteration: Test a faster pressure trigger gated by slow wave direction.

human_read: Weak. It can look too constructive while price remains poor, which is a false-positive problem.
what_got_worse: False-positive control is not good enough for failed steep-downtrend cases.
next_iteration: Bullish fill should require either improving signal structure or less-negative/positive main-signal confirmation.

human_read: Mixed. It eventually shows pressure, but the key question is whether it helps before the double-bottom/run.
what_got_worse: It may confirm after the move starts rather than reveal readiness.
next_iteration: Add separate early curl marker or make fast/slow gap more informative below zero.

human_read: Problem case. It can look constructive even while price structure remains weak.
what_got_worse: It does not punish weak chop and hidden bearish divergence enough.
next_iteration: Bearish/weak context should dominate when price and main signal fail to confirm.

human_read: Too broad to explain the divergence/retest story cleanly.
what_got_worse: It does not expose the user's bearish-then-bullish divergence sequence.
next_iteration: Candidate needs a divergence/weakening-color companion, not only broad pressure.

human_read: Noisy false-positive risk. It looks visually attractive but can treat random sold-off impulses as durable pressure.
what_got_worse: Not enough distinction between true compression and volatile impulse chop.
next_iteration: Add volatility/structure gating or require pressure persistence instead of spike response.
